title: Chicken Seekh Kebab
tagline: OTG sheet-pan chicken mince — spread like a tray, scored, baked with no added oil, wrapped in flaky Malabar paratha.
servingsNote: Bake all 450 g at once; makes 2 wraps.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (450 g raw thigh mince split in 2, one Malabar paratha per wrap, minimal oil, no mayo).
Protein is excellent (~45 g/serving). The bottleneck is fat — it comes from BOTH the chicken thighs and the layered, oily Malabar paratha, not from the cooking method.
To cut ~150–200 kcal per serving: swap the Malabar paratha for a roomali roti or tortilla. Keeping just 1 paratha per wrap (not 2 smaller ones) is already the better macro call.
Chicken mince is the protein base here, so no soya needed. If you want to push to ~50–55 g protein at the same calories, add a little more mince per serving rather than more paratha.

items: 1–2 tsp onion powder (≈ 1 medium onion, but concentrated — start low)
1–2 tbsp curd (adds back moisture the onion powder doesn't)
Optional: 1 tbsp besan or breadcrumbs, if the mix releases water
A little oil, only to wipe the tray so it doesn't stick
1/2 tsp chaat masala or peri-peri sprinkle
Mint chutney or hung curd + spices, as a base spread

steps.title: Mix the mince
steps.body: Combine 450 g chicken thigh mince with 1–2 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 chopped green chilli, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garam masala and 3/4–1 tsp salt. Because onion powder adds flavour but no moisture (unlike a real onion), mix in 1–2 tbsp curd and 1 tsp lemon juice so the kebab doesn't bake dry. If the mix feels wet/loose, work in 1 tbsp besan or breadcrumbs.

steps.title: Rest for binding
steps.body: Let the mix rest 10–15 minutes — binding improves so it crumbles less after baking.

steps.title: Spread & score
steps.body: Line an OTG tray with foil/parchment and wipe it with a little oil. Spread the mince ~1–1.5 cm thick (thicker = rubbery inside, thinner = dries out). Score a square/diamond pattern on the surface — don't cut all the way through. Leave 2–3 mm gaps between sections so heat penetrates and edges brown instead of steaming; run a spoon over the top to smooth cracks.

steps.title: Preheat the OTG
steps.body: Preheat to 220°C for 10–15 minutes, both rods on, tray on the middle rack.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake at 200°C for ~20 minutes total. At the 10–12 minute mark, rotate the tray 180° (OTGs have hot spots) — no flipping needed for a spread tray. If water pools, that's normal for thighs; just extend by 2–3 minutes.

steps.title: Top char
steps.body: Switch to top rod only, move the tray slightly higher, and grill 4–5 minutes for a browned crust. Watch closely — OTGs burn fast. Done = no pink, juices run clear, light crisp edges.

steps.title: Prep the paratha
steps.body: Thaw frozen Malabar parathas 10–15 minutes (don't fully microwave). Dry-roast each side 30–40 seconds on medium, then add a few drops of oil/butter and cook to golden spots with the layers separating. Off the pan, clap it between your hands (or crush lightly) — this separates the layers into that street-style flake.

steps.title: Build the wrap
steps.body: Spread a base of mint chutney or spiced hung curd (don't skip — the paratha and lean-ish chicken are both a bit dry). Cut the baked kebab into strips and lay 150–200 g down the centre without overloading. Add sliced onion, a squeeze of lemon and a sprinkle of chaat masala/peri-peri. Fold the bottom up, roll tight, and toast the rolled wrap on the pan 1–2 minutes for structure.

notes: This spread-and-score method (a sheet-pan mince bake, not shaped skewers) is faster to prep, cooks more evenly and is easier to portion for macros — you trade away the smoky charred edges and classic 'seekh' bite.
Onion powder is ~3× as concentrated as fresh: ~1 tbsp powder ≈ 1 medium onion, but start with 1–2 tsp per 450 g and adjust next time.
Since there's no added oil or mayo, slightly underbake the tray to keep it juicy and lean on the sauce layer and onion + lemon for moisture rather than the chicken.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
